channel.title: about_me on rahuldevajji
channel.description: Recent content in about_me on rahuldevajji

item.description: I am developing an approach to urban heat resilience that bridges the gap between climate science, data-driven research, and community action. My work combines satellite imagery, urban morphology, in-situ and wearable sensor data, and machine learning to model and predict urban heat patterns. I integrate socio-economic, health, and meteorological data to understand the impacts of extreme heat on vulnerable populations. Through projects like Local Climate Zone classification and personal heat exposure studies, I aim to inform equitable and sustainable urban planning solutions.

item.title: rahuldevajji.com
item.description: Rahul Devajji PhD Researcher in Informatics · Healthy Cities Lab · Indiana University Bloomington I study urban heat — how cities concentrate it, who it harms most, and how we can use data to do something about it. My work combines satellite imagery, distributed sensor networks, and machine learning to model hyperlocal temperature and heat vulnerability across cities. Research areas: urban climate · geospatial AI · earth observation · heat–health equity · spatiotemporal modeling
